#japanesevinyl #jazzfusion #jazzlover #masayoshitakanaka #fishmans #casiopea #japanpickups #vinylhaul #recordcollection
transcript: my best pickups from japan weren't fashion related whatsoever some of you may know i'm a massive japanese jazz fusion lover and in terms of accessibility they're just super hard to get your hands on in australia for like under 200 so i spent quite a lot of time at union records when i was in japan and managed to get my hands on a few growl albums for myself and pick up some new stuff firstly we have one of my favorite albums of all time the rainbow goblins by masayoshi takanaka i literally listened to this 60 times on spotify last year and even more across youtube on the live performances it is an absolutely no skip must listen and this one here was secondhand and it was actually an original press from 1981 i believe it is next i think is one of takanaka's most underrated works and it is a smaller ep called jolly jive and this one is just a lot of fun it's very summery um and there's a lot of funk incorporated into the style of this album there's a song in here called taj mahal that's effectively a flip on rod stewart's do you think i'm sexy with a lot of just fantastic guitar and backing vocals added it's better than the original there we have another takanaka record uh alone this one's a bit moody and introspective great range on my man takanaka from there um is one that i really like an artist called sadow watanabe and i just love the album cover on this one frankly i'm not 100 familiar with all the songs on this one but i'm really keen to get into it next up was a group that i was really having a hard time to find uh original impressing of their albums and this one is cassio here notably one of their more famous songs is the pending track on this album uh and yeah absolutely love these guys and finally stepping away from the jazz fusion stuff i found a compilation album of one of my favorite japanese bands fishmans their vibes kind of very dub scar uh easy listening and if you haven't listened to fishmans before i highly recommend getting into some of their works before sitting through the 47 minute light performance of long season you can find that on youtube and i think everyone else i've been in spotify so these were my vinyl pickups from japan that i managed to get back in my suitcase in one piece let me know if you want me to dive into any of these a little bit more i'll be posting some more pickups of like clothes and other that i got while i'm over there so follow along if you like this and want to see more cheers
